_comment: SAMPLE — wargames reads ~/.blumi/settings.json. Copy this file there, replace the PLACEHOLDER keys, and pick a provider under `providers` whose `api_key`/`base_url` you actually have. Fields wargames does NOT parse (everything except providers.*.{api_key, base_url, kind, models}, router.{light,heavy,judge}.{model,provider}, and voice.*) are harmless — they are read by other blumi apps on the same device. See README.md for which fields wargames uses.

minimax._comment: Anthropic-compatible provider used by wargames. The `kind` field tells callers which request envelope to send; wargames always uses the anthropic Messages endpoint regardless of `kind`, so setting `kind: "anthropic"` for any MiniMax/Azure/OpenAI-direct-compatible endpoint is fine. `base_url` must NOT include `/v1/messages` — wargames appends it.

azure-foundry._comment: Alternative Anthropic-compatible endpoint hosted on Azure AI Foundry.

anthropic-direct._comment: Direct Anthropic API as another option. `base_url` is the canonical host — wargames appends /v1/messages.

router._comment: wargames reads ONLY `router.light.{model, provider}` to pick the default Soviet commander model at startup. `router.heavy` and `router.judge` are read by other blumi apps (cyberdeck etc.) and ignored here. The `provider` name must match a key under `providers`.

voice._comment: Optional. wargames never reads this — if present, TTS is best-effort by other apps. Leave the whole block out if you do not want TTS anywhere.
